item.title: Investec Raises $43 Million in Debut Loss-Absorbing Debt Issue
item.description: Investec became the latest South African bank to issue loss-absorbing debt as local lenders work to comply with the new central bank framework.

item.title: Airbnb and Uber Are Competing to Be the Next Big Travel Super App
item.description: Neither company appears content to lead just one category

item.title: Solid Demand to Lift Shipping Rates Underpinned by Supply Strains
item.description: Container capacity is ‘critically tight’ in Asia, allowing carriers to command higher rates

item.title: How Kevin Warsh Might Seek to Bring ‘Regime Change’ to the Fed
item.description: New Chair Kevin Warsh may start reforms with a change to the way the Fed communicates.

item.title: TSMC CEO Pledges 30%-Plus Incentive Bump as AI Profits Soar
item.description: Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Co. chief C.C. Wei told staff they’ll see more than a 30% bump in their profit-sharing payouts this year on average, responding after some voiced concerns over their incentive plans online.

item.title: India’s Richest Civic Body Plans Nearly $1 Billion Muni Bonds
item.description: India’s richest civic authority is planning to raise as much as 95 billion rupees ($992 million) through municipal debt in one or more tranches, marking its foray into the local-currency bond market.

item.title: Temu-Owner PDD’s Revenue Misses as China Competition Persists
item.description: PDD Holding Inc.’s quarterly revenue growth missed expectations after fierce domestic competition offset an improving overseas Temu business.

item.title: Korea’s 100% Stock Run Is Rivaling Nasdaq of 1999
item.description: SK Hynix’s value tops $1 trillion and the Kospi has doubled.

item.title: More Companies Turn to Monitoring Employee AI Skills
item.description: Crypto exchange OKX joins KPMG and other firms changing performance evaluations

item.title: Warner Bros. Tightens $15 Billion Loan Price in Hot Debt Market
item.description: Warner Bros. Discovery Inc. tightened pricing on its loan offering on Wednesday, a day after upsizing the deal to around $15 billion, as investor appetite for corporate credit allows borrowers to set favorable terms.

item.title: Americans Are About to Pay Even More at the Grocery Store
item.description: As Americans confront a surge in prices at the pump, another inflation wave is headed for the grocery store.

item.title: Goldman’s Bell Says European Stocks Offer Breadth in AI Rally
item.description: European equities could attract investors who are worried about the dominance of a handful of technology stocks, according to Sharon Bell, senior European equity strategist at Goldman Sachs Group Inc.

item.title: ECB Says Leveraged Hedge Fund Bets Risk Bond Market Instability
item.description: The European Central Bank warned that the growing role of highly-leveraged trades in the region’s bond markets poses a risk to financial stability.

item.title: Euronext Weighs Revising Data Price Hikes After Pushback
item.description: Euronext NV is considering changes to its planned overhaul of data fees, which critics have said would raise the cost of crucial trading information for some market participants.

item.title: Salesforce Earnings Can Put AI Fears to Bed, Give Stock a Lift
item.description: While software stocks rebound from the artificial intelligence-driven wipeout earlier this year, Salesforce Inc. hasn’t really benefited. But its earnings after the close Wednesday could pull the company’s shares out of their malaise.

item.title: Meloni Told by IMF That Italy’s Energy Aid Must Be More Targeted
item.description: Italian Prime Minister Giorgia Meloni got told by the International Monetary Fund that her government’s energy aid should target poorer families rather than apply to everyone.

item.title: Croatia Picks Zigman to Succeed Vujcic as Central Bank Chief
item.description: Croatia nominated Ante Zigman to lead its central bank, a role that includes a seat on the European Central Bank’s Governing Council.

item.title: Son of Slain Communist Rattles Investors Ahead of Colombian Vote
item.description: The son of a slain communist leader is rattling markets ahead of Colombia’s presidential election, as investors weigh whether he can win — and how radically he would govern if he does.

item.title: Germany, Spain Push Back on Europe’s Plans to Ban Huawei Gear
item.description: Germany and Spain are leading opposition to European Commission plans to ban Chinese technology suppliers from telecom networks as part of new cybersecurity rules, according to people familiar with the negotiations.

item.title: China to Invest $1.1 Billion in Serbia in AI, Robots and Cars
item.description: Chinese companies will pour an additional €940 million ($1.1 billion) to expand their footprint in Serbia, in a major boost to the Balkan country’s economy, President Aleksandar Vucic said.

item.title: Zambia State Mining Firm Strikes Lime Deal With Chinese Operator
item.description: Zambia’s state-controlled mining investment company, ZCCM Investments Holdings Plc, agreed to sell a majority stake in a lime and cement venture to a privately held Chinese company.

item.title: Memory Chip Giant CXMT Heads for China’s Biggest IPO Since 2022
item.description: Memory chip maker ChangXin Memory Technologies Inc. has received approval from the Shanghai Stock Exchange for an initial public offering that’s on track to be the biggest in mainland China since 2022, in a milestone for one of the key technologies of the artificial intelligence buildout.

item.title: Finland’s Unemployment Hits Highest Level in a Quarter Century
item.description: Finland’s unemployment rate jumped to its highest level this century as the Middle East war is denting the recovery in the ailing Nordic economy.

item.title: Germany’s Reiche Tells EU to Avoid Harming Exports to China
item.description: German Economy Minister Katherina Reiche said the European Union should ensure that any measures it applies on Chinese trade don’t harm the bloc’s exports to the country.

item.title: SpaceX Investor Powerlaw to Debut on Nasdaq as IPO Race Heats Up
item.description: Powerlaw Corp., a closed-end fund that owns stakes in Elon Musk’s SpaceX and artificial intelligence firm OpenAI, is going public without raising any capital in the latest public vehicle to cater to retail investors’ clamor for exposure to the hottest private tech companies.

item.title: Prediction Markets Are Booming. So Are Worries About Rampant Insider Trading
item.description: Prediction markets, where users can place bets on real-world outcomes in anything from sports and award shows to elections, interest rate decisions, and even military operations, have become a multi-billion-dollar industry in just a few years. Along the way, they have also become the focus of growing concern that some participants are using privileged information to profit.

item.title: Goldman Strategists Lift S&P 500 Target to 8,000 on AI, Earnings
item.description: Strategists at Goldman Sachs Group Inc. joined peers at Morgan Stanley and Deutsche Bank AG in seeing a 17% return for the S&P 500 Index this year.

item.title: FX Hedging Gains Steam at US, UK Firms as War Sparks Volatility
item.description: Companies in the US and the UK ramped up their currency hedges last quarter as the war in Iran roiled global markets, an industry survey shows.

item.title: Merz Advisers Slash German Growth Forecast, See Faster Inflation
item.description: Economic advisers to German Chancellor Friedrich Merz almost halved their forecast for growth this year as the war in the Middle East and US trade policy weigh on activity.

item.title: ECB Sees Danger of Sudden and Sharp Repricing in Markets
item.description: Financial markets are in danger of a sudden and significant correction, with investors downplaying threats from factors including the Iran war, the European Central Bank warned.
